commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh And honey, and butter, and sheep, and cheese of cows, for David, and for the people that were with him, to eat: for they said, The people are hungry, and weary, and thirsty, in the wilderness. Many translate the Hebrew, "cheese of kine. "(Theodotion) "Calves fed with milk. "(Haydock) Wilderness, where they had been; unless this be placed out of its natural order. (Calmet) The value of a present depends greatly on the time when it is made. (Haydock) "How much dost thou esteem a hospitable reception in a wilderness. "(Seneca, Ben.

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
